expectations.text: Both Bun and Deno are researched (not just one)
expectations.evidence: Response covers both runtimes in depth across all four requested dimensions. WebFetch to bun.sh succeeded for some calls (18 attempts, ~4 succeeded); Deno supplemented with training knowledge.

expectations.text: Sources are annotated with T1-T4 tiers
expectations.evidence: No T1-T4 tier annotations appear in the response. Sources section lists references without tier labels. Response does not distinguish authoritative from secondary sources by tier.
metrics.json: searches_executed: 0. WebSearch denied. 18 WebFetch attempts with 14 errors (4 possible successes to bun.sh). No formal searches executed.

expectations.text: Contradictions or uncertainties between sources are explicitly mentioned
expectations.evidence: Response mentions 'Note: Some Deno-specific data relies on training knowledge' but has no dedicated contradictions section. Trade-offs are discussed structurally (Winner sections) but conflicting source claims are not explicitly surfaced as contradictions.

expectations.text: A recommendation or comparative conclusion is provided
expectations.evidence: Section 8 'Verdict': 'For a greenfield TypeScript backend service in 2025: Bun is the pragmatic choice.' Clear recommendation with specific use-case guidance for both.
